{"query":"New Caledonia dual citizenship agreement ratification status 2025 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1768,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/politics/article/2025/07/12/state-of-new-caledonia-created-in-hard-won-agreement-with-french-government_6743299_5.html","title":"'State of New Caledonia' created in hard-won agreement with French government","content":""It has been agreed to establish a sui generis institutional framework for the 'State of New Caledonia' within the French national entity, enshrined in the Constitution of the French Republic.
New Caledonians will thus benefit from dual nationality, French and New Caledonian.""},{"url":"https://www.lowyinstitute.org/the-interpreter/new-caledonia-s-bougival-accord-offers-path-beyond-independence-deadlock","title":"New Caledonia’s Bougival Accord offers path beyond independence deadlock","content":"A breakthrough in negotiations might finally see progress towards settling the long-running question of New Caledonia’s political status.
The Bougival Accord, signed on 12 July at a meeting convened by French President Emmanuel Macron, falls short of independence for the Pacific territory but does reflect hard-won concessions by all parties.
It creates a new State within France, with unprecedented double nationality, new Caledonian citizenship alongside French citizenship, its own international relations powers (albeit with “respect” for France’s national interests), and processes to allow for the handover of the last remaining sovereign powers over time.
Independence leaders consulted lengthily with their politburo in Noumea on the final evening, meeting Valls at 1AM before a final roundtable at 6AM clinched the deal on 12 July.
Not only will it guarantee more economic prosperity and political stability in New Caledonia itself, after years of deep divisions, political impasse, and in 2024, violence, but it will create a new Pacific state with a voice in international affairs."},{"url":"https://www.aa.com.tr/en/asia-pacific/france-strikes-deal-to-establish-state-of-new-caledonia-under-new-constitutional-status/3629209","title":"France strikes deal to establish ‘State of New Caledonia’ under new constitutional status","content":"Agreement allows for Caledonian nationality, dual citizenship, and revised local governance ahead of 2026 referendum
Political leaders in New Caledonia have signed a deal with the French government to establish a new political entity known as the “State of New Caledonia,” which will be enshrined in the French Constitution, French media reported Saturday.
Paris pledged continued support for the territory’s economic development, with a particular focus on New Caledonia’s nickel industry — a key part of its economy and a long-standing point of local concern.
A local referendum on the new status is planned for February 2026."},{"url":"https://highseasalliance.org/treaty-ratification/table-of-countries/","title":"Signature and Ratification Progress Table - High Seas Alliance Treaty Ratification","content":"For the High Seas Treaty* to have an impact on the water, it must be signed and ratified by at least 60 countries, a milestone reached in September 2025, enabling the Treaty to enter into force on 17 January 2026.
However, as a regional economic integration organization, only the ratifications of its Member States count toward the 60 required for the Agreement’s entry into force, in accordance with Article 68(3) of the Treaty.
***Denmark’s ratification does not apply to Greenland and the Faroe Islands."},{"url":"https://www.ejiltalk.org/a-new-future-for-new-caledonia/","title":"A new future for New Caledonia? – EJIL: Talk!","content":"The accord expands the New Caledonian citizenship into a nationality."}]}

The new referendum will not be on independence.","score":0.98591,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.jurist.org/news/2025/07/france-signs-agreement-establishing-a-state-of-new-caledonia","title":"France signs agreement establishing a 'State of New Caledonia' - JURIST - News","content":"France signs agreement establishing a ‘State of New Caledonia’ News. **France signs agreement establishing a ‘State of New Caledonia’**. Representatives of France and the New Caledonian political forces signed an agreement on Saturday establishing a “State of New Caledonia” within the French republic. The agreement, titled “The bet on trust,” provides for the enshrinement of the “State of New Caledonia” in the French Constitution, offering New Caledonia a path to stability and international recognition. In 1998, the Nouméa Accord established a gradual decolonization process over a 20-year period, entailing shared sovereignty between New Caledonia and France, a new dual citizenship for New Caledonians, and full recognition of the indigenous Kanak identity. The agreement also provides for the adoption of the Fundamental Law of New Caledonia in 2026, enshrining its capacity for self-organization, and its power to modify the country’s identity symbols, and incorporate a charter of New Caledonian values.","score":0.98351,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A8g7GzRzSv4","title":"New Caledonia secures path to special status within France","content":"The deal introduces dual citizenship, expanded autonomy, and economic reforms- pending a 2026 local referendum and could set a precedent for","score":0.97996,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.aa.com.tr/en/asia-pacific/france-strikes-deal-to-establish-state-of-new-caledonia-under-new-constitutional-status/3629209","title":"France strikes deal to establish 'State of New Caledonia ...","content":"Agreement allows for Caledonian nationality, dual citizenship, and revised local governance ahead of 2026 referendum | Anadolu.","score":0.97472,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/politics/article/2025/07/12/state-of-new-caledonia-created-in-hard-won-agreement-with-french-government_6743299_5.html","title":"'State of New Caledonia' created in hard-won agreement ...","content":"Under the agreement, New Caledonians will benefit from French and New Caledonian dual nationality. in February 2026, In March-April 2026, a","score":0.96765,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.41,"request_id":"5d9c216d-b969-42ce-ba7f-6399d3308027"}
